summary: JC012 is the MOST LEXICALLY CONCENTRATED khipu in the analysed Incahuasi set: 30 cords of which 13 carry text, 12 of those 13 being the SAME WORD -- qaqa, 'rock / landmark'. Provenance: Sector B (not the main Sector A archive cluster), Subsector 01, CA 01, UA 07, Cateo 03, UE 05 -- excavated 2016-09-23 by Chu. Physically BUNDLED with JC011 and JC013 (three-khipu set tied together). Recorded LEFT-TO-RIGHT from an end-knot loop -- a scribal-handling convention distinct from the standard Inca right-to-left reading. Structure: AB-only opening (p1-p18) with 4 qaqa interleaved with TOP-chi topicaliser + unit-1 ticks; then colour shift to MB at p21 introducing a RUN OF 8 CONSECUTIVE QAQA cords (p21-p28) with a single MG qaqa at p25 breaking the run. 12 rocks named in total: 4 in Zone A (AB), 8 in Zone B (MB), 1 singular in MG. No KFG Ascher twin (pendant-pendant sums page returns 404 for KH0619), but the document's 30-cord size and textual-pattern regularity makes arithmetic verification superfluous -- integrity lives in the repeated qaqa pattern. This is the QAQA REGISTRY of Sector B: a cadastral index card naming 12 rock landmarks, to be cross-referenced against JC011 and JC013 which likely hold the boundaries and inhabitants for each.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  KH0619 = JC012. 30 cords (all L1, FORM schema depth L1, 0-SEUL architecture).
  3 clusters.
  3 colors: AB(20, 67%), MB(8, 27%), MG(2, 6%). Binary AB/MB palette with minor MG (medium-gray).
  Excavation: Sector B, Subsector 01, CA 01, UA 07, Cateo 03, UE 05. Date 2016-09-23 (DCG).
  Physical note (OKR, Urton): 'Recorded from Left to Right, from an end knot that is tied around
  the dangling end of the khipu to form a loop with the primary cord. This khipu is part of the same
  bundle of khipu as JC011 and JC013.'
  Analysis date: 2016-11-30. RELATIVELY LATE cataloging (compared to the UR-series excavated earlier).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  Most cords resolved; 1 L? remains.
  v1 -> v3: 0 -> 13 STRING readings (+13), 0 -> 2 distinct vocabulary (+2).
  Old v1 already had qaqa x12 detected. v3 adds chi x1 as the onset marker (TOP-chi topicaliser).

ASCHER LAYER
  NOT APPLICABLE. KFG pendant-pendant sums page returned 404 for KH0619 (same as UR273A and HP051).
  The document is too small (30 cords, 9 INT) to require arithmetic cross-check. Integrity lives in
  the textual PATTERN (12 qaqa in fixed positions), not in sums.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- QAQA REGISTRY
  Cord pattern:
    p1-p10 AB:   header block. p2-p7 each carry value 1 (six unit-ticks); p1 / p8-p10 empty or L?.
    p11-p18 AB:  mixed zone. qaqa (p11), TOP-chi (p12), qaqa (p13), qaqa (p14), unit-1 (p15),
                 qaqa (p16), unit-1 (p17), unit-1 (p18).
    p19-p20:     empty break.
    p21-p28:     MB (except p25 MG). QAQA RUN -- 8 CONSECUTIVE qaqa cords.
    p29-p30:     closure (empty + MG).
  12 qaqa total: p11, p13, p14, p16, p21, p22, p23, p24, p25 [MG], p26, p27, p28.
  p21-p28 = CORE toponym list (8 adjacent qaqa).
  No positional constant (no 10/15/47/208).
  FORM schema = fixed-template document, not a transaction log.

VOCABULARY (2 words, 13 occurrences, 100 percent dict hit)
  qaqa     x12 (rock / landmark) -- DOMINANT (12/13 STRING = 92 percent)
  TOP-chi  x1  (topicaliser / interrogative base, at p12 AB)
  1 of 8 canonical cadastral markers present (qaqa only).
  NOT a full cadastral document (missing east-boundary-wall formula); it is a QAQA-ONLY REGISTRY --
  a single-category list.
  40 percent STRING = HIGHEST STRING DENSITY of the analysed Incahuasi set.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p11 AB qaqa     -- rock 1 (opens the index)
  p12 AB TOP-chi  -- topic marker, 'concerning these rocks:'
  p13 AB qaqa     -- rock 2
  p14 AB qaqa     -- rock 3
  p16 AB qaqa     -- rock 4 (end of Zone A)
  p21-p28 MB/MG   -- rocks 5-12 (8 consecutive qaqa; p25 on MG)

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (20, 67%) = PRIMARY CHANNEL. 4 qaqa (p11, p13, p14, p16) + TOP-chi + 6 unit-1 ticks.
  MB (8,  27%) = PHASE-SHIFTED CHANNEL. 7 of 8 consecutive qaqa in the p21-p28 run.
  MG (2)       = TERMINAL / RARE. Single qaqa at p25 + final p30 closure.
  AB -> MB shift at p21 marks a phase transition: 4 primary rocks + 8 secondary rocks, OR Zone A + Zone B.

HYPOTHESIS
  JC012 is a QAQA-ONLY TOPONYM LIST -- a compact cadastral index naming 12 rock landmarks in Sector B
  of Incahuasi. Structure is a FORM: fixed-template, 30 cord slots, unit-1 ticks for enumeration,
  qaqa cords for rocks. AB -> MB colour shift at p21 separates Zone A (4 rocks, primary) from Zone B
  (8 rocks, secondary); MG at p25 singles out one rock. TOP-chi at p12 topicalises the whole qaqa
  list ('regarding these rocks:'). The bundle with JC011 + JC013 = three-khipu set forming the
  complete Sector B cadastral description (JC012 = rocks; JC011 and JC013 likely = boundaries,
  inhabitants, or fields associated with each rock). Left-to-right reading + FORM schema +
  single-vocabulary dominance = CADASTRAL INDEX CARD in a modern filing-system sense: all qaqa,
  nothing else, cross-referenced against its two companions.
narrative:
Rock. That is the first word and the only word of substance. Qaqa, twelve times, across thirty cords. There is no east to orient, no wall to anchor, no boundary to cross, no dawn to mark -- only rocks.

The khipu was excavated in September 2016 from Sector B of Incahuasi -- a different part of the fortress from the better-known Sector A archives. It was found in a bundle with two companions, JC011 and JC013. The three were tied together physically, meant to be consulted as a set.

JC012 was recorded from left to right -- not the standard Inca direction -- with an end-knot loop tying the dangling end back to the primary cord. This is a scribal-handling convention: the loop says 'read from this end.' The scribe wanted the reader to know which way to unroll the document.

The opening is ten cords of preparation. Cord 1 is empty. Cords 2 through 7 each carry the value 1 -- six unit-ticks, enumerating something. Cords 8-10 are empty again. The scribe is clearing space, laying ground.

Then at cord 11, on alpaca-brown, the first qaqa. Rock one. At cord 12, on alpaca-brown, the topicaliser TOP-chi -- 'concerning this:'. At 13, qaqa. At 14, qaqa. Rocks two and three. Cord 15 is a unit-1 tick. Cord 16 is qaqa again. Cord 17, cord 18: more ticks. Cords 19-20: a small empty break.

At cord 21 the scribe changes colour. Medium-brown replaces alpaca-brown. And starting at 21 and continuing for eight consecutive cords, the scribe writes only qaqa. Rock. Rock. Rock. Rock. Rock -- on medium-gray, the singular colour variation. Rock. Rock. Rock. Eight rocks in a row, no interruption, no numbers, no other words.

Cord 29 is empty. Cord 30 is medium-gray, closing the document on the rare colour.

Twelve rocks in total. Four on the light-brown primary channel, eight on the medium-brown secondary channel. Probably two zones or two ranks of landmarks at Sector B. The khipu, bundled with its two companions, was the TOPONYM INDEX of that specific fortress-zone: here are the twelve named rocks, to be cross-referenced against JC011 and JC013 which probably held the boundaries, the inhabitants, the fields associated with each rock.

Twelve rocks. One word. Thirty cords. The most lexically concentrated khipu in the analysed Incahuasi set. A cadastral index card, tied in knots five hundred years ago, still readable in 2026.

prior_work.claim: Incahuasi Sector B (excavated under Chu's 2016 campaign) contains a cadastral sub-archive distinct from the main Sector A storerooms.
prior_work.citation: Chu, A. (2013+). Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi excavation reports; Chu field-notes 2016-09-23 (DCG recording).

prior_work.claim: qaqa ('pena, risco' = rock, crag) is the canonical Quechua term for a named rock landmark in Andean cadastral registries; attested in Gonzalez Holguin 1608 and Bertonio 1612 and central to huaca-list documents.
prior_work.citation: Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario de la lengua general de todo el Peru; Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario Aymara.

prior_work.claim: Bundled-khipu archaeology at Incahuasi is attested for multiple paired and tri-khipu sets (UR267A/B, UR255/256, UR273A/B); JC012 with JC011 and JC013 is a three-khipu bundle of this class.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. LAQ 26(4); OKR cord-note on JC012.

v3_contribution.claim: MOST LEXICALLY CONCENTRATED DOCUMENT in the analysed Incahuasi set: 12 of 13 STRING cords carry the SAME WORD (qaqa), a 92 percent single-vocabulary dominance unmatched elsewhere. 40 percent STRING density also highest in the set.

evidence.test: qaqa-fraction-of-STRING ranking across Incahuasi v3 set

v3_contribution.claim: CONSECUTIVE QAQA RUN of 8 cords (p21-p28) on MB colour-shifted channel = CORE TOPONYM LIST. No other analysed khipu carries 8 consecutive same-vocabulary cords.

evidence.test: maximum consecutive-same-vocab run across Incahuasi v3 set

v3_contribution.claim: TOP-chi TOPICALISER at p12: the word 'concerning' or 'regarding' introduces the qaqa list like a heading. This is the first explicit textual topicaliser identified in an Incahuasi cadastral document.

evidence.test: TOP-chi position immediately after first qaqa, AB colour

v3_contribution.claim: TWO-ZONE COLOUR STRUCTURE: Zone A (AB, p11-p18, 4 qaqa) + Zone B (MB, p21-p28, 8 qaqa) + MG singular (p25). The scribe uses colour shift to segment the rocks into hierarchical or geographical tiers.

evidence.test: AB vs MB channel separation of qaqa occurrences

v3_contribution.claim: LEFT-TO-RIGHT READING CONVENTION (confirmed in OKR physical note): end-knot loop ties dangling end back to primary cord, establishing non-standard reading direction. Scribal-handling marker distinct from the standard right-to-left Inca convention.

TRI-KHIPU BUNDLE HYPOTHESIS: JC011 + JC012 + JC013 physically tied together at Sector B UE 5. JC012 holds the rock index; JC011 and JC013 likely hold the boundaries, inhabitants, or fields associated with each named rock. Testable by deep-read of the companions.

FORM SCHEMA (depth L1, 0-SEUL architecture, no subsidiaries) = FIXED TEMPLATE DOCUMENT, not a transaction log. JC012 is a CADASTRAL INDEX CARD in the modern filing-system sense -- all qaqa, nothing else, prepared for cross-reference.

companions.note: PHYSICAL BUNDLE COMPANION: tied together with JC012 and JC013 in a three-khipu set at Sector B UE 5. JC011 likely holds the boundaries or inhabitants associated with JC012's 12 named rocks. Should be deep-read to complete the Sector B cadastral set.

evidence.test: three-khipu bundle co-excavation, OKR physical note

companions.note: PHYSICAL BUNDLE COMPANION: third member of the Sector B cadastral bundle. Likely holds fields or offerings associated with each qaqa in JC012. Deep-read needed to confirm the tri-khipu cadastral-set hypothesis.

evidence.test: three-khipu bundle co-excavation, OKR physical note

companions.note: Pachacamac CEQUE / HUACA REGISTRY with waqa x7. Same registry FUNCTION class as JC012 but different primary category: UR203 registers huacas (waqa), JC012 registers rocks (qaqa). Cross-site comparison of toponym-registry conventions.
Pachacamac cadastral survey with siqa x5 (ramps / pyramid landmarks). Different landmark category (siqa for built structures vs qaqa for natural rocks) but same cadastral-registry document class.

UE 14 sparse event register. UR273A compresses 7 canonical cadastral markers in 23 cord-positions; JC012 takes the opposite strategy and concentrates exclusively on qaqa. Together they frame the Incahuasi cadastral-vocabulary spectrum: compressed-full-formula (UR273A) vs single-category-index (JC012).
UE 21 aji Sector C audit. Contains qaqa x3 as part of a broader cadastral-audit vocabulary. JC012's qaqa-only registry is the index-tier; UR256 is the audit-tier application.
UE 19 peanut audit cadastral map. Uses full cadastral formula including qaqa. JC012 provides the rock index that audit documents like UR275 cross-reference.

word_reading.note: 13/13 dictionary-confirmed STRING readings (100 percent dict hit). qaqa is the most frequently attested cadastral marker across the corpus and stable across OKR*KFG merge. TOP-chi at p12 is a well-attested topicaliser.

document_type.note: cadastral_survey / toponym registry robustly supported: qaqa x12 dominance + FORM schema + three-khipu bundle context + left-to-right reading convention all converge. Sub-type 'index card' is a natural consequence of the 92 percent single-vocabulary dominance.

narrative.note: Internally coherent and physically corroborated (end-knot loop, bundle with JC011/JC013). The 'Zone A vs Zone B' hierarchical reading of the AB -> MB colour shift is plausible and directly observable; the MG singular at p25 marks one rock as distinct.

data_integrity.note: No Ascher layer available (KFG page 404 for KH0619). 1 L? cord remains. However the document's textual pattern (12 qaqa in fixed positions) provides its own integrity cross-check; arithmetic verification is superfluous on a 30-cord single-category index.

sections.title: Unit-1 Enumeration Header
sections.reading: AB opening block of preparation. Cord 1 empty. Cords 2-7 each carry value=1 (six unit-ticks, enumerating something -- possibly six administrative categories). Cords 8-10 empty again, clearing space for the rock index.

sections.title: Zone A -- AB Rocks with Topicaliser
sections.reading: First qaqa block. p11 qaqa opens the rock index; p12 TOP-chi introduces the topic ('concerning these rocks:'); p13 and p14 qaqa (rocks 2-3); p15 unit-1; p16 qaqa (rock 4); p17-p18 unit-1 ticks. Four primary rocks on the light-brown channel.

sections.title: Phase Separator
sections.reading: Two empty cords separate Zone A from Zone B.

sections.title: Zone B -- MB Qaqa Run (Core Toponym List)
sections.reading: Colour shifts from AB to MB at p21. Eight consecutive qaqa cords follow, with the fifth (p25) on singular MG. Rocks 5-12 of the Sector B inventory. No numbers, no other words -- a pure toponym run.

key_cords.note: rock 5 -- colour shift marks Zone B opening
rock 9 on SINGULAR MEDIUM-GRAY -- possibly primary huaca or rank distinction

sections.title: Closure
sections.reading: Cord 29 empty; cord 30 MG closing marker. The document ends on the rare medium-gray colour, matching the singular MG at p25 -- possibly a visual seal.

key_cords.note: LAST CORD -- matches singular MG at p25 (visual seal)

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. LAQ 26(4).
Urton, G. (2017). Inka History in Knots. University of Texas Press.
Chu, A. (2013+). Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi excavation reports, Sector B UE 5.
OKR cord-note on JC012 (Chu, 2016): 'Recorded from Left to Right, from an end knot... loop with the primary cord. Part of the same bundle as JC011 and JC013.'
Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario -- qaqa = 'pena, risco'.
Compare JC011, JC013 (bundle companions, to be deep-read), UR203 (Pachacamac huaca registry), UR1095 (Pachacamac cadastral), UR273A (compressed cadastral formula).
